round_trip.symmetry: ASYMMETRIC — forward transfer is stronger than reverse in all cases
round_trip.interpretation: The mapping is DIRECTIONAL. Domains with richer arithmetic structure (genus2 Selmer, EC torsion) predict simpler invariants (NF class number) better than the reverse. This is expected: more information -> less information is easier than the reverse.

out_of_distribution.verdict: GENERALIZES — OOD performance EXCEEDS in-distribution for EC->NF (203%!). The transfer is STRONGER at high Megethos.
out_of_distribution.interpretation: At high complexity (large conductors), the Arithmos signal is CLEANER. This is consistent with asymptotic behavior of arithmetic invariants — they become more regular at large N. The phoneme space captures a structure that improves with scale.

combined.symmetry: FAILS — mapping is directional (rich->simple easier than simple->rich)
combined.honest_summary: The translation layer is real but DIRECTIONAL. It works best predicting simpler invariants from richer ones, and generalizes to unseen complexity ranges. It is not a symmetric isomorphism between domains — it is an information-compressing projection. This is actually the correct behavior for a coordinate system: projections are naturally lossy and directional.
